title: Citrusy Jelly Argentinian Olives Tagine
description: A festive Argentinian dish featuring glassy jelly and olives in a traditional cataplana, perfect for special occasions. Serve warm, garnished with fresh herbs.
instructions: Heat oil in a large cataplana over medium heat.
Add sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 8 minutes.
Add sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 4 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and cinnamon, and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Add water, grapefruit and lemon juice, honey, and chopped fenugreek leaves.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Add pitted olives and cook for an additional 10 minutes, until olives are tender.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Transfer cataplana to an ice bath to shock, and let cool completely.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh herbs.
Reheat gently before serving, if desired.
